title: skopeo copy progress output is written to stdout
body:
I want to be able to copy to stdout so that it can be piped into another command:

```bash
skopeo copy docker://docker.io/library/registry:3 docker-archive:/dev/stdout \
  | ssh someserver skopeo copy docker-archive:/dev/stdin docker-daemon:docker.io/library/registry:3
```

This will allow me to install an image on a system that cannot reach out to the official registry where the image lives.  However, the progress information also gets written to stdout which interferes with the intentional output of the docker-archive transport:

```bash
ltheisen@server ~
$ skopeo copy docker://docker.io/library/registry:3 docker-archive:/dev/stdout | head -n 5
Getting image source signatures
Copying blob sha256:f18232174bc91741fdf3da96d85011092101a032a93a388b79e99e69c2d5c870
08000c18d16dadf9553d747a58cf44023423a9ab010aab96cf263d2216b8b350.tar0000444000000000000000003676500000000000000020332 0ustar0000000000000000bin/0000755000000000000000000000000014753475154010033 5ustar0000000000000000bin/arch0000777000000000000000000000000014753475154013135 2/bin/busyboxustar0000000000000000bin/ash0000777000000000000000000000000014753475154012773 2/bin/busyboxustar0000000000000000bin/base640000777000000000000000000000000014753475154013304 2/bin/busyboxustar0000000000000000bin/bbconfig0000777000000000000000000000000014753475154013771 2/bin/busyboxustar0000000000000000bin/busybox0000755000000000000000000305341014742516561011456 
...
```

If the progress was switched to stderr it would not interfere with the intentional output.  I know that i can work around this by supressing progress with `--quiet`, but then i lose the useful progress information.

comments.body:
Thanks for reaching out. Compare #2590; this is just not something we can change, any more.

It would have to be a new option.

---

(Also, `docker-archive` is not a format that can represent all images or all features, nor can it preserve image digests / signatures, and it has a significant performance cost. Using a proper mirroring solution would almost certainly pay off. Even `skopeo copy docker://… dir:… && scp … && skopeo copy dir:… $dest` would be better, although not optimal.)

@mtrmac a new option would be sufficient...  something like `--progress-on <string>: one of stdout or stderr (default: stdout)`.  feels a little odd to have to do that though.

I just followed over to #2590, sorry i missed that upon initial filing... i will close this out as already answered.
